blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Fine. I'll give you one last chance. Defeat Savina and Closer first, and then you can face me. I also defeated Boyle, so you won't complain, right? If you can beat those two, I'll take on you then.
Closer and Savina cheered.
This set the stage for Dante.
The stage was now set for Dante.
Of course, they had no intention of losing to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No. There's no need for that.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Hey, get a grip. You're the challenger here. If you want to face me, you need to at least...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why don't all three of you come at me at once? I want to get this over with and not worry about it anymore.
You madman…!
Dante and his crew's eyes widened.
Even at the Royal Magic Academy, they had never received such an insult.
Nade, watching the situation, clicked his tongue.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Shirone sure knows how to push people's buttons. I'd never want to get into a verbal fight with him.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: He's got a real talent for pissing people off. It's not that he doesn't do it, he just chooses not to.
Iruki, who had said this, voluntarily stepped into the tense scene.
He had been holding back because Shirone hadn't gotten involved, but he wasn't the type to enjoy being ignored.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Hey, Shirone. If it's something this fun, count me in.
Dante glared fiercely at Iruki, who had barged in like an intruder.
It was clear that he was trying to force this feud into a team battle since he didn't think Shirone could handle it alone.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Iruki, this isn't your fight. I can see right through your scheme.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: What are you talking about? This is right up my alley. I'll take all of you on at once. If I calculate one second per person, it'll take exactly one seconds.
Closer snorted.

blocks.speaker: Closer
blocks.content: Idiot, if it's one second per person, it should be three sec....
He frowned as he realized the meaning behind Iruki's words.

blocks.speaker: Closer
blocks.content: These guys really! Are you crazy? Who do you think we are, acting so cocky!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: If you're so confident, why don't you come at us one by one? The conclusion is simple: win the fight, and that's it. What's so complicated about it? Right, Dante?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: You're misunderstanding something. It's not about wearing Shirone down by making him fight up the ranks. It's about proving you're qualified to challenge me.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: We are well qualified. If you defeat Shirone, there will be no one who will dare challenge you anymore.
Iruki pointed to the students observing the situation here.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: See? No need to overthink this. If you beat us, you're the best. You'll have control of the advanced class. And that must be the special mission someone gave you.
Iruki's words hit the mark.
Having been at the Royal Magic Academy, he likely understood Olivia's strategy.
Even if he didn't, he surely sensed that Olivia's involvement was the reason for their aggressive behavior.
With the cards now on the table, Dante no longer hid his intentions.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Fine. Let's do a team battle. To avoid disrupting classes, we'll have an unofficial match over the weekend. We'll have one person fight each week, and the first to win two matches wins. If you lose two matches first, then there's no need for me to fight Shirone—I'll automatically be the winner. Do you accept?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Fine. If you want to keep up appearances until the end, we'll do it that way. But I'm going to win anyway, so you won't have to worry about avoiding a fight with Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Hmph, you talk big. In that case, add one more person to your side. It doesn't matter who—bring Boyle if you want.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That wouldn't be any fun. We have our own lineup, you know.
Iruki turned his head.
Nade pointed at himself with a bewildered expression.
Helping Shirone wasn't the issue, but he hadn't expected to be dragged into the battle.
However, refusing now would dampen Shirone's momentum, so he had no choice but to go along.
Dante tilted his head as Nade walked toward him.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Nade? Are you sure about this?
Based on what he'd observed, Nade didn't have impressive grades or standout performance in practical evaluations.
Since Dante had been tasked with crushing Shirone, taking down everyone associated with him seemed like a better option.
Nade, who arrived at the battlefield, whispered to Iruki.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Hey, what are you thinking? What if I lose?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Just fill in the numbers. I'll take care of everything.
While the two whispered, Dante went over to the teachers.
Since they'd been listening to the entire conversation from the start, there was no need for further explanation.

blocks.speaker: Sienna
blocks.content: We'd like to settle this. I request that the Heaven's Trial practice field be opened for us this weekend.
Sienna spoke as the representative.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: It's not our decision to make, but we'll consider it. Go back and wait.
The students stopped their practice and waited anxiously.
Shirone's challenge was a huge event, but if the school didn't approve, the match would be canceled.
When all the dedicated teachers of Heaven's Trial gathered, Sienna spoke.

blocks.speaker: Sienna
blocks.content: So, it has come to this. Should I say that it was bound to happen at some point?

blocks.speaker: Thad
blocks.content: I disagree. We can't let students judge each other's mistakes. We should hold a disciplinary committee to announce the harm done to Pandora.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Hmm, I partially agree with Teacher Thad. But calling it 'judgment' seems too harsh. Can't we see it as a form of self-regulation among the students?

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: I'm not against the importance of autonomy, but this is a duel. There's something strange about the way students fight each other with magic.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Even so, Boyle and Dante have already settled their match. Many students are dueling as we speak. I see opening Heaven's Trial on the weekend as no different from holding supplementary classes.

blocks.speaker: Sienna
blocks.content: Since opinions are divided, we need to address the practical issue. If we refuse, Dante will likely appeal to Principal Olivia. What do you think her decision will be?
A sigh escaped from Thad's throat.
Olivia would certainly approve it.
He couldn't fathom why, but it seemed this was her intention all along.
Moreover, Alpheas had instructed them to follow her lead without question.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Because Principal Olivia trusts Dante. And Principal Alpheas trusts Shirone. In hindsight, this isn't just a problem between students.

blocks.speaker: Thad
blocks.content: I want to trust Shirone too. Just as I'm a teacher and a monk, Shirone, though a student, embodies the spirit of a monk who seeks righteousness. Perhaps knowing this, Principal Alpheas gave full authority to Principal Olivia?
Thad was lost in thought.
Thinking back now, it did seem odd.
No matter how extraordinary his mentor was, it seemed impossible to invite someone as esteemed as Jerome Olivia, the kingdom's top teacher, to the academy.
There must have been a significant reason behind it.

blocks.speaker: Thad
blocks.content: How is it that you cause more accidents than your disciples, Master?
Thad sighed and walked over to the students.
Then he announced the preliminary results regarding Dante's request.

blocks.speaker: Thad
blocks.content: As a result of the emergency meeting, we've decided to approve the opening of Heaven's Trial for the unofficial interpersonal combat training.
The students erupted in cheers, so loud that Thad's additional comment about needing higher approval was completely drowned out.
Not that it needed to be mentioned anyway.
Wow! This is a major event!
They're finally going to face off! Shirone and Dante! Dante and Shirone! Woohoo!
The students, unable to contain their excitement, jumped around wildly.
In the midst of the uproar, Iruki tapped people's shoulders one by one, pointing to himself.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Hey, it's not just Shirone who fights, I fight too.
Waaaah! Iruki! Did you hear? Shirone and Dante are going to face off!
News of the confrontation spread throughout the academy through word of mouth from students.
In the shadowy circles of research groups, flyers detailing information about the two contenders were already being distributed, fueling speculation.
The teachers' conference room was also buzzing with discussions.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: I'll say it again: this is dangerous. Allowing students to settle personal grievances through physical conflict goes against our policies.

blocks.speaker: Thad
blocks.content: But, Principal! If this continues, the academy's reputation...

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: It's not about fighting; it's about determining who's the best. After all, most of them will be in the graduation class next year or the year after. You can see it as an intensification of competition.

blocks.speaker: Thad
blocks.content: But, Principal! If this continues, the academy's reputation...

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: The battle between Shirone and Dante will be an event that will draw the attention of the entire kingdom. I will also personally suggest to the Teachers' Association that they send a special correspondent.
The conference room became quiet.
Having the inspector of the Teachers' Association personally call for a special correspondent meant that Alpheas Magic Academy would gain considerable attention.
Furthermore, if it was published in an academic journal with Dante, the star of the kingdom, at the forefront, it would be a great privilege for the academy.
Sweet visions danced in the minds of the teachers—of Alpheas Magic Academy becoming the kingdom's premier institution.
The best teacher there to guide students (that is me).

blocks.speaker: Thad
blocks.content: Principal Olivia, may I say something?

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Thad, proceed as planned.

blocks.speaker: Thad
blocks.content: Yes, let's proceed as is… Huh?
Thad turned his head, his face flushed.
Alpheas was standing at the back door.
Given the gravity of the situation, even Olivia hadn't dismissed him this time.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: As expected, Teacher Alpheas has great foresight. This event will bring tremendous merit to the academy; it would be foolish to let it slip by.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: I wonder if it'll work out? Thanks to you, I gain fame in my later years. Hahahaha!

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Once the duel is over, there will be widespread reports of Dante's victory. The child named Shirone will be deeply hurt, but seeing you so gleeful almost makes me pity him.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Hahaha! Both children attend Alpheas Magic Academy, so they are essentially my students. But I also believe that the best must be determined.
Olivia's eyes narrowed.
She was irritated by how Alpheas countered every word she said.
This time, even Alpheas' gaze was fierce.
The two teachers, who had spent their lives in education, were now confronting each other with their philosophies, both having found students who could mark the culmination of their careers.
An hour later, the proposal for the unofficial duel was formally approved.
Magic Clash (1)
The students eagerly awaited the upcoming weekend.
This was the first event in nearly ten years at Alpheas Magic Academy that had created such a stir.
Rumors spread that even a special correspondent from the Teachers' Association would be sent to witness the duel between Shirone and Dante.
The boys joked about pulling off outrageous pranks to make their mark on the world, while the girls fretted over what to wear.
Iruki and Nade headed to their research group.
As they walked the short distance, they heard countless stories, though they couldn't tell which were true or false.
However, none of the stories were about them.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I didn't expect much from the beginning, but now that it's happening, it feels a bit deflating. Are we just sideshows?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: At least the correspondent is coming. Um... do you think it would be better if I went first?
Nade had been worried since the day the match was approved.
With Closer eyeing Iruki, it was likely that his opponent would be Victor Savina.
A fearsome woman who accelerates with Haste and sprays Wind Cutters in all directions.
Excluding the graduating class, she was a student who could be ranked in the top 20 in the kingdom, so it was clear that it would be a tough fight regardless of the matchup.
